fix: restrict group collapse/expand to chevron click only

Prevent accidental collapse when clicking elsewhere on the group
header (e.g. after editing a cell). Only the chevron arrow button
triggers toggle now.

design: minimal type-aware empty states for all editable cells

Replace verbose "Cliquer pour modifier..." with quiet type-specific
indicators: dash for text/number, persistent icons for date/phone/
email/location, muted User icon without text, "—" for empty selects.
Icons always visible as anchors so empty→filled transition is smooth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: replace DossierFilters with ColumnFilters on dossiers page

Extract matchesFilter to shared utility, update dossiers page to use
the same per-column filtering system as board pages. Remove old
DossierFilters component (status + date range only) in favor of
ColumnFilters (type-aware operators for all column types).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

design: add dark/light theme system with next-themes

Install next-themes, add ThemeProvider + ThemeToggle, define CSS
design tokens for both modes in globals.css, migrate ~1500 hardcoded
colors to semantic tokens across all components. Sidebar is light in
light mode, dark navy in dark mode. All color-coded elements (badges,
file types, status indicators) use dark: variants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add dedicated landing pages for all 4 services

Create conversion-optimised service pages at /services/ai-strategy,
/services/intelligent-automation, /services/solution-design, and
/services/notion-systems. Each page follows PAS framework with hero,
problem, solution, process, audience, FAQ, and CTA sections. Update
products page to link service cards to their dedicated pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: conversation modes + public API

- ModeEngine state machine with per-mode logic, scoring, and dynamic prompts
- ModeVoiceAgent in agent_factory with llm_node() system prompt swapping
- conversation_results table for structured result persistence
- Dashboard: 6-button mode selector + per-mode config panels (questions, scoring, catalog)

Public API:
- API keys with vai_ prefix, SHA-256 hashing, scopes, and expiration
- X-API-Key auth middleware with scope-based access control
- Versioned /api/v1/ endpoints (calls, contacts, agents, conversation-results, webhooks)
- Webhook delivery with HMAC-SHA256 signing and 3x retry
- Dashboard: API tab with key management and webhook configuration

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: increase WebRTC initial outgoing bitrate to 15 Mbps

The previous 8 Mbps initial estimate caused mediasoup's congestion
control to throttle video packets when IPTV streams exceeded 8 Mbps,
resulting in periodic video freezes while audio continued. 15 Mbps
provides headroom above the 12 Mbps video maxrate cap.

commit.message:
fix(vpn): set VPN source IP on split-routing table for correct NAT

Packets routed through wg0 kept their original source IP (10.0.2.15
from enp0s1) instead of the VPN address (10.5.0.2). The NordVPN peer
drops packets from unexpected source IPs. Adding src to the route
ensures outgoing packets use the wg0 address.

commit.message:
caching CSR attributes (#14444)

* tests/bench: add repeated property-access benchmarks for x509 types

The existing load benchmarks create a fresh object each iteration, so
the cache is always cold and caching optimisations show no benefit there.
Add benchmarks that construct the object once and then repeatedly call
the getter, exercising the warm-cache path:

  Certificate : subject, issuer, public_key(),
                signature_hash_algorithm, signature_algorithm_oid
  CRL         : issuer, serial-number lookup (hit and miss)
  OCSPRequest : issuer_name_hash, issuer_key_hash,
                hash_algorithm, serial_number (all in one bench)
  OCSPResponse: issuer_key_hash, serial_number,
                signature_hash_algorithm (all in one bench)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Bokovoy <abokovoy@redhat.com>

* x509/csr: cache attributes getter result

Wrap the attributes getter in PyOnceLock so the expensive loop over
ASN.1 attributes (OID conversion, PyBytes allocation, Attributes
construction) runs at most once per CertificateSigningRequest object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Bokovoy <abokovoy@redhat.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Alexander Bokovoy <abokovoy@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add dynamic position sizing + RSI 65/35 threshold

Position sizing based on ML confidence:
- 55-65%: 1 contract (58.6% WR)
- 65-70%: 2 contracts (80.2% WR)
- 70%+: 3 contracts (93.1% WR)

Backtest shows +71% PnL with only +8% DD increase.

Changes:
- RSI thresholds: 75/25 → 65/35
- TradersPost webhook now includes quantityType + quantity
- Account-specific limits (MGC capped at 2 for Lucid, no MGC on Apex)

commit.message:
Fix incomplete [N. [and]] parser gap in Pass 1 and fallback stripping

Batch C fixed classifyBracket and the normalizer for [N. [and]] but
missed two spots in parseInstruction:
- Pass 1 altContentMap only matched [or] signals, never built forward
  pointers for [and] signals → altText fell through to fallback
- Fallback stripping regex only removed [or] prefix, leaving the full
  "N. [and]" signal text in the rendered alt output

Updated both regexes to (?:or|and). All 36 tests pass.

Initial commit: AI chief-of-staff API backend (Slack-free)

Extracted core AI agent, services, and tooling from the Slack-based
assistant into a standalone REST API. Adds Google OAuth-based session
auth, CORS middleware, and web_notify stubs to replace Slack calls.

commit.message:
feat(failover): Bloc 2 — naming convention + 3 nouveaux pollers

Convention naming — alignment sur paths Ubuntu :

1. hive_writer.py : retire "ohlcv_1m" de _DATASET_AMBIGUOUS
   - Bybit/OKX kline pollers utilisaient dataset="ohlcv_1m" → incorrectement
     normalisé en "ohlcv_1m_perp|spot", alors qu'Ubuntu écrit dataset=ohlcv_1m
     (normalize_dataset Ubuntu ne qualifie pas "ohlcv_1m", seulement "ohlcv")
   - Correction : bybitf/okxf + ohlcv_1m → provider=bybit/dataset=ohlcv_1m ✓

2. reconciler.py : même correction _AMBIGUOUS_DATASETS

3. Hyperliquid source=dex (pre-existing bug) :
   EXCHANGE_TO_SOURCE_PROVIDER["hyperliquid"] = ("dex", "hyperliquid") sur Ubuntu
   mais tous les pollers failover passaient source="cex" (défaut).
   Fix : source="dex" dans hyperliquid_book/trades/funding/kline pollers

Nouveaux pollers :

4. bybit_spot_kline.py — OHLCV 1m Bybit Spot (category=spot)
   provider="bybits" → bybit, dataset="ohlcv_1m" → ohlcv_1m (inchangé)
   Chemin : source=cex/provider=bybit/dataset=ohlcv_1m/symbol=.../...

5. okx_spot_kline.py — OHLCV 1m OKX Spot (instId sans -SWAP)
   provider="okxs" → okx, dataset="ohlcv_1m" → ohlcv_1m (inchangé)
   Chemin : source=cex/provider=okx/dataset=ohlcv_1m/symbol=.../...

6. hyperliquid_oi.py — OI via POST metaAndAssetCtxs
   Champs : open_interest, mark_price, funding_rate par coin
   source="dex", provider="hyperliquid", dataset="oi"

Orchestrator : 29 → 32 pollers (BybitSpotKline, OKXSpotKline, HyperliquidOI)

* Add unified inventory.py script; rename output files per hub slug

- Add readiness/inventory.py: single public script that dynamically fetches
  all DPLA hub names, loops through each, skips fully-partnered hubs
  (upload=true), runs the full inventory pipeline, and saves per-hub
  Markdown reports. Auto-updates README.md when a new hub is first run.
  API key via DPLA_API_KEY env var; inventory_data.json used as shared cache.
- Rename readiness/indiana_inventory.md → indiana_memory_inventory.md and
  readiness/heartland_inventory.md → heartland_hub_inventory.md to match the
  {hub_slug}_inventory.md naming convention used by inventory.py.
- Add readiness/inventory_data.json to .gitignore (cache file, not versioned).
- Update readiness/README.md: fix filenames in Inventories table; add sections
  documenting inventory.py usage and inventory_data.json structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify ListOfClockingDeclAssignMultiple → §14.3 [skip ci]

The test exercises the `list_of_clocking_decl_assign` grammar production (Syntax 14-1), which allows multiple comma-separated signal names in a single clocking block direction declaration (input a, b, c). This production is defined directly within §14.3 "Clocking block declaration" with no deeper subclause.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Instantly integration: proper campaign setup, stop AI credit burn, clean send architecture

- ensureInstantlyCampaign() now queries Instantly for real sending accounts,
  creates campaign with email_list, passthrough template ({{subject}}/{{body}}),
  business hours schedule, and activates it
- Remove conflicting dual send path: leads added one at a time by
  processSequenceQueue() with AI content, not bulk-pushed without content
- Pre-flight check: verify active campaign exists BEFORE generating AI copy
  (prevents burning Claude credits when no delivery route exists)
- Backoff: 1 hour when no campaign, 30 min on failed step (was 5 min loop)
- First-boot: create Instantly campaign after contact conversion, before first tick
- One-time repair: reset deferred enrollments once campaign is available
- Fix channel mismatch: enrollments use "email", webhook accepts "email" or "multi"
- Fix contact source: "apollo" not "instantly" in prospector
- Fix Stripe: convert deal dollars to cents for invoice amount
- AI copy: remove sign-off from generated emails (Instantly appends real sender signature)

Add confirmation prompt and dedupe check before writing calendar events (#6)

  so all flights can be displayed before anything is written

  '✈️ JetLag Plan' within each flight's plan date range

  printing a clear "already exists" message
